what_it_does: Defines implementation rules and section-specific build steps for a deep-rebuild loop, covering edge typing, gallery decomposition, content plumbing, and product-face verification.
observations: States standing laws for every iteration, including verify-by-use, file-altitude before edit, versioning, canon homes, declared edges, and post-iteration proof requirements.
Section E describes how to formalize face fields and declare typed edges in dna/types.json with locators and checks.
Section M lays out a plain-script decomposition of index.html into ui/gallery modules while preserving DOM, boot order, and behavior.
Section C moves authored content into a content/ registry and adds resolver/rendering plumbing for story-tree-backed pieces.
Section P defines product-face verification expectations around design tokens, screenshots, rooms, mobile layout, and lint cleanliness.
standouts: Verification is treated as a required operational step after each iteration.
Edge declarations require verb, opposite, kind, checker, and instance locators.
Gallery decomposition is framed as behavior-preserving extraction rather than redesign.
Content is defined as resolver input outside dna/ with compatibility through the types door.
conventions: Uses uppercase action markers such as BUILD, MODIFY, REFERENCE, KEEP, and DON'T.
Separates work by lettered sections tied to implementation domains.
Uses imperative process language with explicit sequence steps and verification gates.
Treats file moves, version bumps, manifest entries, and lint checks as part of the implementation contract.
concerns: Many requirements depend on named external files, synthesis rounds, and line ranges that are not explained in this file.
Several long paragraphs combine policy, implementation steps, and verification criteria, which may make execution tracking harder.
Some verification outputs are named but their evidence format is not defined in this file.
notes: Briefing note: this file is the procedural guide for rebuilding typed edges, gallery modules, content plumbing, and product-surface checks under strict verification rules.
questions: What are the exact six proofs expected from ./engine/rebuild.sh?
What artifact format should record screenshot comparisons, plant-error tests, and design-critic passes?
Where are the referenced synthesis rounds and line ranges documented?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it explains how to make completion criteria true through laws, sequences, file actions, and verification checks.
apparent_themes: Authored content separated from identity data
intention_signals: It seems intended to guide iterative implementation work so structural, content, typing, and design changes remain traceable, declared, versioned, and tested by use.
novelty: The guide combines implementation sequencing with governance rules such as edge laws, canon homes, plant-error tests, and per-iteration rebuild proof gates.
contribution: Provides a procedural ledger of implementation laws, edit targets, sequencing constraints, and verification obligations that a fused system would keep as process and validation guidance.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for a deep-rebuild loop covering standing iteration laws, typed edge declaration, index.html gallery module extraction, content registry/rendering plumbing, and product-face verification.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to turn completion criteria into actionable, verifiable implementation steps while preserving behavior, provenance, and design-system constraints.
